zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Beauchamp於1274年7月25日出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特哈契時，他的父親John de Beauchamp11271歲，母親Cecily de Vivonne17歲。他於大約1301年與Joan Chenduit結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭薩默塞特被列為governor of bridgwater。他去世於1336年10月12日在英國英格蘭享年62歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特斯托克 素布 哈姆頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Beauchamp于1274年7月25日出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特哈契时，他的父亲John de Beauchamp11271岁，母亲Cecily de Vivonne17岁。他于大约1301年与Joan Chenduit结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他在英国英格兰薩默塞特被列为governor of bridgwater。他去世于1336年10月12日在英国英格兰享年62岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特斯托克 素布 哈姆頓。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Beauchamp was born on 25 July 1274, in Hatch, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Beauchamp, was 11271 and his mother, Cecily de Vivonne, was 17. He married Joan Chenduit about 1301. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as governor of bridgwater in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. He died on 12 October 1336, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Beauchamp est né le 25 juillet 1274, à Hatch, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Beauchamp, avait 11271 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Vivonne, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Joan Chenduit environ 1301. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est governor of bridgwater à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 12 octobre 1336, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Beauchamp am 25. Juli 1274 in Hatch, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Beauchamp 11271 und seine Mutter Cecily de Vivonne 17. Er hat um 1301 Joan Chenduit geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als governor of bridgwater in Somerset, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 12. Oktober 1336 im Alter von 62 Jahren in England, United Kingdom und wurde in Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Beauchamp è nato il il 25 luglio 1274 nel Hatch, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de Beauchamp, aveva 11271 anni e il suo madre, Cecily de Vivonne, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Joan Chenduit circa 1301. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come governor of bridgwater nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 12 ottobre 1336, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Beauchampが1274年7月25日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトハトチで生まれた時，父John de Beauchampは11271歳，母Cecily de Vivonneは17歳であった。約1301年にJoan Chenduitと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリスソメルセトでの職業はgovernor of bridgwaterと記録されている。彼は1336年10月12日にイギリスイギリスで62歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトストク スブ ハムドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Beauchamp(이)가 1274년 7월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 해트치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Beauchamp(은)는 11271세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecily de Vivonne(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1301년 Joan Chenduit(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 governor of bridgwater이었다. 그는 1336년 10월 12일 영국 잉글랜드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 스토크 수브 함던에 묻혔다.

pt.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Beauchamp nasceu em 25 de julho de 1274, em Hatch, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Beauchamp, tinha 11271 anos e sua mãe, Cecily de Vivonne, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Chenduit aproximadamente 1301. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como governor of bridgwater em Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 12 de outubro de 1336, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ro.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Beauchamp родился 25 июля 1274 г. в местности хач, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Beauchamp было 11271 год, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Vivonne, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Chenduit около 1301 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии governor of bridgwater в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 12 октября 1336 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности сток суб хамдон, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Beauchamp nació el 25 de julio de 1274, en Hatch, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Beauchamp, tenía 11271 años y su mamá, Cecily de Vivonne, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Joan Chenduit aproximadamente 1301. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era governor of bridgwater en Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 12 de octubre de 1336, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Inglaterra.
